incorrects.description: Student selected 4 × 2: reversed the number of groups and items per group

params.description: First box highlights, then second box highlights.

steps.text: You're thinking about the numbers, but the order matters. The first number tells how many groups. Count the boxes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 4: confused multiplication with addition

params.description: All items in both boxes highlight.

steps.text: Not addition. We have equal groups here. When we have the same amount in each group, we multiply.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 + 2: confused multiplication with addition and reversed the numbers

params.description: Boxes pulse one at a time.

steps.text: Look at the equal groups. Two boxes with four in each. That's multiplication, not addition.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation counts the 2 boxes, then counts the 4 items in one box.

steps.text: Let me show you. First, count the groups. Two boxes. Then count what's in one group. Four items. So it's 2 times 4. The answer is 2 × 4.
